review_summary: The segment has enough concrete transfer-learning and debugging guidance for two grounded questions without cutting a separate clip.

question_bank.question: You have a target dataset that is similar to ImageNet but small. Which transfer-learning strategy best matches the recommended guidance?
choices: Freeze a pretrained backbone and train only a new classifier on top
Train a deep CNN from scratch with random weights regardless of data size
Discard pretrained features and use only handcrafted color histograms
Use grid search first and postpone model training until later
question_bank.explanation: When the new data is similar to the source domain but limited in size, pretrained features are already useful. Freezing the backbone and learning a simple classifier on top is a strong low-data baseline.

question_bank.question: What was recommended as the first debugging step when a training setup seems broken?
choices: Overfit a tiny sample and drive training loss near zero
Switch from random search to exhaustive grid search
Add more data augmentation before checking the loss curve
question_bank.explanation: If a model cannot overfit a very small sample, something basic is wrong in the pipeline, loss, optimization setup, or implementation. Solving that simple case first makes later debugging much easier.
